description: Active status epilepticus on EMS arrival in a 28-year-old.

patient.name: David K., 28M
patient.chief_complaint: Witnessed generalized tonic-clonic seizure activity for over 8 minutes continuous. Status epilepticus. Two doses of IM midazolam given by EMS without resolution. No previous seizure history reported.
patient.history: Unknown - presumed first-time seizure per witness

rubric_notes: Status epilepticus by definition (continuous seizure >5min OR failure to recover between seizures). Critical vitals: HR >130, RR >20 concerning, SpO2 <90 critical, GCS 3 critical. Airway at risk (post-ictal aspiration, hypoventilation). Per RUBRIC §3 status epilepticus = ESI 2 minimum; with airway compromise (SpO2 <90 + GCS 3) elevates to ESI 1. Lorazepam IV + phenytoin loading + airway management.
